title: Slow-cooker honey and mustard chicken
subtitle: Boneless thighs in a sticky honey mustard sauce
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs slow-cooked in a honey, Dijon mustard, and wholegrain mustard sauce with garlic and stock for six hours. The sauce reduces to a sticky, rounded glaze around the chicken. One of the simplest and most reliable slow-cooker chicken dishes.
sourceNotes: Honey and mustard chicken became a British weeknight standard from the 1980s, typically pan-fried or baked. The slow cooker version followed naturally — the sauce is stable at long, low temperatures and the sweetness from the honey deepens over the cook without burning.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well. Reheat gently to avoid the sauce splitting.

content.text: This is the simplest recipe in the chicken section. Everything goes in raw and the sauce makes itself over six hours. The honey caramelises slightly against the sides of the pot, the mustard mellows, and the garlic rounds out. There is no browning step and no fond to worry about.

Mix the {{honey}}, {{dijon-mustard}}, {{wholegrain-mustard}}, {{garlic}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} with the {{stock-chicken}} in the slow cooker. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}} and turn to coat. Tuck in the {{thyme-fresh}}. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the chicken is tender and the sauce has reduced to a sticky consistency. Remove the {{thyme-fresh}} sprigs, taste and adjust seasoning.

items.cause: Too much stock, or chicken released a lot of liquid
items.fix: Transfer the cooking liquid to a small saucepan and reduce over high heat for 5 minutes until sticky. Pour back over the chicken

items.cause: Honey quantity too high for taste
items.fix: A squeeze of lemon juice and a teaspoon more Dijon mustard balances excessive sweetness. Taste and adjust
Boneless thighs are forgiving, but 7 hours is the outer limit. Set a timer. If they have dried slightly, add an extra 50 ml stock and stir

content.text: Honey and mustard chicken is a family weeknight dinner that children eat without complaint and adults find deeply satisfying. The slow cooker version is faster to prepare than the oven version. Serve with rice, mashed potato, or new potatoes and a green vegetable.
